core_mechanism: confidence-bound-based sequential allocation over a finite set of actions (a stochastic multi-armed bandit) that balances exploration and exploitation via concentration-inequality confidence intervals to achieve logarithmic cumulative regret against the best fixed action, matching the Lai-Robbins lower bound
brief_reason: The abstract formulates choosing the interval k as a stochastic multi-armed bandit and proposes Hoeffding- and Bernstein-based lower-confidence-bound algorithms achieving O(K log T) regret matching the Lai-Robbins bound, which is exactly the confidence-bound exploration-exploitation trade-off underlying bandit allocation.
